---
title: "Discussion"
---

## TODO

- **Depth quantization.** Why per-pixel depths only land on even integers (passes
  15 and 16 re-observe identical pointings + dithers, so the sum is always 2×k).
- **Field coverage non-uniformity.** Only ~25% of skycells within 0.5° of (10, 0)
  reach the design depth. The 0.5° cone is not the right way to think about the
  footprint — it's a union of three visit-cluster patches plus the gaps.
- **Kron flux bias.** The +0.17 mag under-measurement is a Kron-photometry
  property on extended sources; unbiased recovered flux for galaxies would use
  larger aperture (e.g. `aper16_flux`) or segment flux. Relevant for downstream
  science.
- **Zodiacal background.** Measured 1.28 e⁻/pix/s, ~3.4× the polar-minimum
  reference. Expected for a field at ecliptic latitude −4° observed at
  elongation 128°. Already near the best achievable for this target — the
  field is structurally on the ecliptic, and the sim date is near the best
  observable elongation.
- **Grism implications.** Discussion of what the recovered per-skycell depth
  and completeness mean for the grism observations the campaign is intended
  to support.
